question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: Based on the March 2026 CPI report, the Consumer Price Index increased only 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis in March, well below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. Despite earlier predictions of a large energy-driven spike due to Middle East conflict, the actual monthly increase was significantly lower than the market-implied probability of 30%.
key_factors: March CPI increased only 0.3% monthly vs 0.9% threshold
Energy price impacts were less severe than predicted
Annual inflation rate reached 2.8% but monthly change was modest
risks: Potential confusion between different CPI measures
